verses.text: With the wall built and gates hung, Nehemiah appoints gatekeepers and singers, and sets faithful Hanani and God-fearing Hananiah over the city, for he was 'a faithful man, and feared God above many.' The city is large but the people few. Trust is given to those who fear God most; reverence is the best qualification for sacred charge.

With the wall built and gates hung, Nehemiah appoints gatekeepers and singers, and sets faithful Hanani and God-fearing Hananiah over the city, for he was 'a faithful man, and feared God above many.' The city is large but the people few. Trust is given to those who fear God most; reverence is the best qualification for sacred charge.

With the wall built and gates hung, Nehemiah appoints gatekeepers and singers, and sets faithful Hanani and God-fearing Hananiah over the city, for he was 'a faithful man, and feared God above many.' The city is large but the people few. Trust is given to those who fear God most; reverence is the best qualification for sacred charge.

With the wall built and gates hung, Nehemiah appoints gatekeepers and singers, and sets faithful Hanani and God-fearing Hananiah over the city, for he was 'a faithful man, and feared God above many.' The city is large but the people few. Trust is given to those who fear God most; reverence is the best qualification for sacred charge.

God puts it in Nehemiah's heart to gather the people and reckon them by genealogy, and he finds the register of those who first returned. The good purpose is owned as God's prompting. The LORD stirs the hearts of His servants to the very plans that serve His people.

God puts it in Nehemiah's heart to gather the people and reckon them by genealogy, and he finds the register of those who first returned. The good purpose is owned as God's prompting. The LORD stirs the hearts of His servants to the very plans that serve His people.

God puts it in Nehemiah's heart to gather the people and reckon them by genealogy, and he finds the register of those who first returned. The good purpose is owned as God's prompting. The LORD stirs the hearts of His servants to the very plans that serve His people.

The families, priests, Levites, and temple servants who first returned are numbered house by house. God keeps the roll of His people. Every household that answered the call to come home is recorded, for the LORD forgets none who seek Him.

Certain priests cannot find their genealogy and are barred from holy things until a priest should arise with Urim and Thummim. A claim to serve must be proven. God guards the holiness of His altar, that none should presume to it without right.

Certain priests cannot find their genealogy and are barred from holy things until a priest should arise with Urim and Thummim. A claim to serve must be proven. God guards the holiness of His altar, that none should presume to it without right.

The whole assembly numbers over forty-two thousand, besides servants, singers, and beasts of burden. A great company is restored. From a scattered, captive people God gathers a nation again, none too small to be counted.

The governor and the chief fathers give freely to the work — gold, basins, and priestly garments for the treasury. Leaders give first and give generously. Willing gifts from those at the head encourage the whole people to support God's house.

The governor and the chief fathers give freely to the work — gold, basins, and priestly garments for the treasury. Leaders give first and give generously. Willing gifts from those at the head encourage the whole people to support God's house.

So the priests, Levites, gatekeepers, singers, and all Israel settle in their cities. The people come home to their inheritance. God restores not only His house but a place for every family that returns to Him.
